sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Cagliari have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, managing 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss, with a notable victory against Torino (2-1). Milan, on the other hand, are in strong form with 3 wins, 1 draw, and 1 loss in their recent 5 games, including a convincing 3-0 win over Hellas Verona. This suggests Milan carry momentum into this fixture.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, both matches ended in draws (1-1 and 3-3), indicating competitive games with both teams finding the net. Milan have historically dominated Cagliari, remaining unbeaten in their last 15 head-to-heads, which could play a psychological role.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Milan, sitting 2nd in the league with 35 points, boast a strong attack (27 goals scored) and a solid defense (13 conceded). Cagliari, positioned 14th with 18 points, struggle defensively (24 goals conceded) and lack consistency in creating chances, ranking low in key attacking metrics. Milan's superior stats tilt the odds in their favor.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Milan's Christian Pulisic (8 goals, 7.37 avg rating) is a standout threat, though they miss Rafael Leao. Cagliari rely on goalkeeper Elia Caprile (7.88 avg rating in last 5 matches) for defensive solidity, but the absence of Michael Folorunsho may hurt their midfield.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Milan's form, league position, and historical edge, they are favored to win, aligning with Sofascore's 55% away win probability. Cagliari's recent scoring form and home advantage could make it competitive, but their defensive frailties might be exploited by Milan's attacking prowess.
